verdict_reasoning: B2M is a non-anchored structural partner of MHC class I, synthesized in the cytosol and reaching the cell surface exclusively via non-covalent binding to the MHC heavy chain. It has no intrinsic membrane-anchoring mechanism (no TM, no GPI, no lipidation) and is in equilibrium with a large soluble pool. The only surface-association route is pMHC-complex assembly, which is excluded from surface-accessibility triage per protocol. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic targets B2M itself. B2M qualifies as secreted_only (more specifically, pMHC-partner-intracellular): the protein body is not independently surface-accessible.
